jobs.description: We're a fast-growing women's wellness brand doing multiple 7-figures every month on Meta. Three SKUs, all on Shopify. We scale hard on the front end. Now we need someone to fix the back end. Our business model is built on 2-month breakeven, which means every subscriber who stays past month 2 is pure profit. Right now too many don't. Most customers stay 2 months and leave. That breaks our model. I'm looking for one operator to own the entire retention engine. Not an email marketer. Not an agency. A real retention lead who can move renewal rate, save rate, LTV, and chargebacks, with proof. This seat owns subscriptions, LTV, retention, and everything that happens after the customer clicks "buy." The problem: -Average customer stays 2 months. We need that number up, minimum 5 months. -Renewal rate is too low (around 60%). Target is 75%+. -Save rate on cancel is working but needs to push higher. -Target is 15%+ across all SKUs. -Chargeback rate is at 0.37% but target is under 0.30%. -Customer portal needs a full rebuild, portal per product, cancellation per product, not one global flow. If you can fix this, this is the seat. If you can't, don't apply. What you'll own on day one: -Cancel flow optimization in Loop, keep pushing save rate past 15% -Subscription retention strategy, discount ladders, pause logic, swap to bundles, skip cycles -Customer portal rebuild, portal per product, cancel per product (not global) -Chargeback reduction, pre-rebill flow, refund SOP, dispute automation -Post-purchase experience, insert cards, first-refill delight, etc. -Loyalty program, build it from scratch -LTV analysis + cohort reporting -Klaviyo flows, pre-rebill, winback, reactivation, welcome, post-purchase, everything about subscriptions -Klaviyo campaigns, weekly newsletters, promos, drops, education series -First 30 days = 100% balm subscription. After that, all three SKUs. The goal: -Get customers staying 5+ months, not 2 -Renewal rate to 75%+ -Save rate to 15%+ -Chargebacks under 0.30% -LTV up across every cohort Our business model is 2-month breakeven. Every extra month a customer stays = pure profit. You're not just fighting churn. You're compounding LTV. How we work here (read this carefully): -We move fast. Speed beats perfection every time. -We ship weekly. Not quarterly. Weekly. -We test everything. No decisions on vibes. Data or strong opinions loosely held. -We pull from the best. Always studying brands ahead of us. Magic Spoon. Liquid IV. Oats Overnight. Gruns. Everyday Dose. IM8. Primal Queen. Ryze. We get the idea and we test FAST. -Proactive first. I'll send you ideas to test when I see things, and you'll do the same for me. But don't wait on me to feed the pipeline. You're running it. -Straight talk. If you don't know, say so. If you disagree, push back. Don't hedge. Don't corporate-speak me. If any of that sounds annoying to you, we won't last a week. Who you are: -You've worked brand-side at an 8 or 9-figure subscription DTC brand. Supplements or wellness is a plus. If your resume is all client work or one-time purchase brands, close the tab. -Bonus if you've also worked SaaS retention, the cohort math and LTV modeling translates. But you must have DTC sub experience first. SaaS-only = skip. -You know Loop Subscriptions cold (or you've run Recharge at real scale and can transition). -You've moved real retention numbers, churn, save rate, renewal, LTV, and you can prove it with before/after data. -You understand trial-to-subscription models. You know the 2-month breakeven math. -You read A/B tests. You design them. You call them when they're done. You document the learnings. -You've built a loyalty program before or know exactly how you'd build one. -You're fluent in English, written and spoken. You study the best DTC subscription brands obsessively. Bonus (huge plus): You're AI-native. You use ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity, or whatever's current to move 10x faster than people who don't. You're ahead of the curve, not catching up to it. If you're using AI to write flow copy at scale, segment customers, automate cohort analysis, personalize cancel offers, or build faster, tell us exactly how. This isn't a requirement. It's an edge. The person who has it is the person who wins this seat. Who you are NOT: -Agency-only, never worked brand-side -Email agency owner who'll pitch their team instead of themselves -"Email marketer" who sends newsletters, we need a retention strategist who owns subs -Info product or coaching background -Never worked on subscription DTC -SaaS-only with no consumer subscription experience -Someone who needs hand-holding -Someone who measures hours instead of output If any of these are you, skip. jobs.description: We're a Danish e-commerce marketing agency running paid traffic (Meta, TikTok, Google) for 20+ Shopify brands. We're looking for an ongoing tracking freelancer who can handle a steady stream of funnel setups — paid per funnel, not hourly. 🟢 The setup we need tracked: For many of our clients, we run sales funnels that look like this: → Paid ad (Meta / TikTok / Google) → Advertorial or listicle (hosted on Webflow, styled as if it's from the Shopify store for credibility) → CTA button to the Shopify product page → Checkout / purchase The Webflow-to-Shopify handoff is where tracking breaks for most people. We need clean, reliable attribution across the full path — ad → advertorial → product page → purchase — so we can actually see which creatives and funnels drive sales. 🟢 What we're looking for: - 3+ years with Shopify tracking (GTM, pixels, server-side, customer events) - Hands-on with Stape.io (sGTM containers, Conversion APIs, server-side setups) - Proven experience tracking across separate domains (Webflow landing → Shopify store) without losing attribution - Solid grip on Meta CAPI, TikTok Events API, Google Enhanced Conversions 🟢 Bonus: You've set up tracking for advertorial/listicle funnels before and know exactly what breaks You're filling the one gap that's costing us attribution clarity — nothing more, nothing less. 🟢 How it works: - You pitch us a fixed price per funnel setup - We start with 1–2 client funnels as a test - If it clicks, we have a steady pipeline — new funnel setups regularly, paid per setup 🟢 To apply — answer these 4 questions (generic applications get ignored): - Have you set up tracking across two separate domains (e.g. Webflow landing → Shopify store) before? Walk us through exactly how you solved it. - How do you handle Meta CAPI / TikTok Events API when the user journey starts on a domain that isn't the Shopify store? - Share one tracking setup you're proud of — what was the challenge, and how did you solve it? - What's your fixed price per funnel setup for a standard Webflow→Shopify case with Meta + TikTok + Google tracking?
jobs.title: E-commerce Tracking Specialist — Webflow→Shopify Funnel Tracking (Ongoing, Fixed Price Per Setup)

jobs.description: We run an online store and export our order data monthly from Shopify into Excel. The data is messy — inconsistent product names, duplicate orders, missing customer regions, and mixed date formats. We need a freelancer to clean the dataset, organise it properly, and build a clear sales dashboard we can use for monthly reporting. The dataset covers 12 months of orders (~1,500 rows) and includes: order ID, date, customer name, product name, category, quantity, unit price, discount, total revenue, sales channel (website, Amazon, social), and shipping region. Scope of work: — Import and clean the raw CSV export using Power Query — Fix inconsistent product names, remove duplicates, standardise date formats — Add calculated columns: net revenue after discount, month, quarter, channel group — Build a monthly revenue trend with MoM % change — Analyse top 10 products by revenue and units sold — Break down sales by channel (website vs Amazon vs social) — Identify low-performing products and high-return months — Build an Excel dashboard with slicers for Month, Category, and Channel Deliverables: — Cleaned Excel file with Power Query steps intact (so we can refresh it next month) — Product performance table (top/bottom performers) — Channel breakdown summary — Monthly revenue trend with chart — Dashboard with slicers — one-screen, no scrolling — Short written summary: what sold, what didn't, and where to focus next quarter Required skills: Microsoft Excel · Power Query · Data cleaning · Pivot Tables · Chart design · Attention to detail Preferred: Experience with Shopify or e-commerce order data · Understanding of sales KPIs (AOV, conversion, return rate) · Ability to deliver a clean, non-technical summary.
jobs.title: Dashboard for E-commerce reporting

jobs.description: Title: Shopify Theme – Implement Complete Website Design (Theme Editor First, Step-by-Step Brief Provided) Hi, I'm looking for a reliable Shopify expert to implement my complete website design into my Shopify store. ⚠️ IMPORTANT: This is NOT a design or copywriting job. TIMELINE: - Brief will be delivered within 24h after hire - Expected completion: by Tuesday, April 28, 2026 (latest: Wednesday, April 29) REQUIREMENTS: - Minimum 95% Job Success Score - At least a few completed Shopify projects - Portfolio with at least 3 live Shopify stores (please share links) - Experience with section-based premium themes (Canyon, Impact, Prestige, Symmetry) SCREENING QUESTIONS (please answer in your proposal): 1. jobs.description: Got you. Here’s the full spec you can hand to a developer. This gets old stock onto Marketplace under your business name only, keeps your assistant off your personal account, and doesn’t touch your current ads. Project Brief: Segment Shopify Clearance Stock to Facebook Marketplace Goal: List clearance/old stock on Facebook Marketplace under Page name. No personal profile involvement. No impact to existing Advantage+ ads. --- PART 1: Shopify Setup - Hide Clearance From Main Store + Ads Create hidden collection Shopify Admin → Products → Collections → Create collection Name: Clearance - Hidden Type: Manual Conditions: None Search engine listing: Click "Edit" → Uncheck "Search engines can see this page" Sales channels: Uncheck "Online Store" so it doesn't show in navigation Add products: Add all old stock items here Tag all clearance products Shopify → Products → Bulk edit → Add tag: clearance Also add tag: main_store to ALL non-clearance products Why: Lets us auto-segment in Meta later. Stop clearance syncing to main Facebook catalog Shopify → Settings → Apps and sales channels → Facebook & Instagram → Settings → Product catalog → Publishing → Include products: Custom → Condition: Product tag IS main_store → Save Result: Your current Advantage+ ads keep running from Main Catalog. Clearance is excluded within 24h. --- PART 2: Meta Business Setup - New Clearance Catalog Create separate clearance catalog business.facebook.com/commerce_manager → Catalogs → Add Catalog → Create New Catalog → Type: E-commerce → Name: Clearance Only AU → Connect to: Select your Business Portfolio + Page Connect Shopify as data source for clearance only Commerce Manager → Clearance Only AU catalog → Catalog → Data Sources → Add Items → Partner Platform → Shopify → Connect → Choose: "Select specific collections" → Tick "Clearance - Hidden" only → Default product category: Set to match your goods → Import schedule: Hourly Set sales channel visibility Commerce Manager → Clearance Only AU catalog → Settings → Sales Channels Marketplace: ON Facebook Shop: OFF Instagram Shopping: OFF Ads: OFF for now - we'll create separate ads later Turn OFF Marketplace for Main Catalog Commerce Manager → Your existing Main Catalog → Settings → Sales Channels Marketplace: OFF Facebook Shop: ON Instagram: ON Ads: ON This stops new stock from appearing on Marketplace. --- PART 3: Permissions - Give Assistant Access Without Personal Profile Add assistant to Business Portfolio business.facebook.com/settings → People → Add Email: [assistant email] Employee access: ON Assign assets: - Catalog → Clearance Only AU → Manage - Commerce Account → Manage - Page → Messages + Content access only DO NOT give: Ad Account, Main Catalog, Business Settings, Personal profile Assistant workflow after this: She logs into: business.facebook.com Sees only: Commerce Manager → Clearance Only AU + Page Inbox Can: Add/edit clearance products, reply to Marketplace messages Cannot: See your personal profile, touch ads, see main products --- PART 4: Marketplace Listing Settings Configure Commerce Account Commerce Manager → Settings → Business Assets → Commerce Account → Business Info: Add GST number, business address in Sydney → Payouts: Connect bank account or PayPal → Shipping: Create profile "AU Standard" → $10 or Free over $50 → Return policy: 30 days, buyer pays return → Customer service email: support@[yourdomain].com Set checkout method for clearance Commerce Manager → Clearance Only AU → Settings → Checkout Method Recommended: "Message to buy" for fastest setup Alternative: "Checkout on another website" → Link to Shopify with auto-discount code --- PART 5: Optional - Run Ads for Clearance Only Only do this AFTER steps 1-4 are live Ads Manager → Create → Sales campaign → Advantage+ Catalog ON → Catalog: Select "Clearance Only AU" → Product set: All products → Audience: Remarketing OR Broad AU → Budget: $10-20/day to start This will NOT affect your existing ASC because it uses a different catalog. --- PART 6: QA Checklist Before Handoff | Check | Where to look | Pass condition | | **Name on Marketplace** | Incognito → http://facebook.com/marketplace → Search test SKU | Shows "[Your Store Name]" not personal name | | **Ads isolation** | Ads Manager → Existing ASC → Product set | Still uses Main Catalog only | | **Assistant access** | Business Settings → People → Assistant | Sees Clearance catalog only, no Ad Account | | **Shop tab clean** | Facebook Page → Shop tab | No clearance items visible | | **Messages routing** | Send test message to Marketplace listing | Lands in Page inbox in Meta Business Suite | --- Developer Notes + Gotchas Time to live: Meta takes 24-72h to approve Commerce Account + first Marketplace listings. Category rules: Used/refurbished/digital goods banned on Marketplace AU. "Last season new" is fine. Inventory sync: Shopify → Clearance catalog is 1-way. If item sells on Marketplace, manually set to 0 in Shopify to prevent oversell. GST: Add "Price includes GST. ABN: XX XXX XXX XXX" to product descriptions for AU compliance. Images: Marketplace crops to square. Use 1:1 images or Meta will auto-crop badly.

The pages are already built and structured using a third party app. This is not a build from scratch - we need someone who can come in, think like a CRO specialist, execute like a developer, and finish like a designer. This is a staged project. Stage one is the coming soon page only. We want to see how you work, how you think, and what you deliver before we hand you the rest of the site. Perform well here and the rest of the project will follow (stage two will require knowledge of Shopify Subscriptions). STAGE ONE SCOPE — Coming Soon Page 1. Functional Fix (priority) The email and SMS capture form on the page was AI-generated and doesn't currently function correctly. We need: - Email field to capture subscribers directly into Shopify - SMS opt-in to work alongside email as a single form submission - Both to integrate as one customer record in Shopify with email and SMS marketing consent captured correctly 2. Branding & Design Pass (IMPORTANT) The page is structurally solid but unbranded. Complete brand guidelines will be provided. Every section needs to feel premium, conversion-focused, and visually stunning - not just technically correct. Colours, typography, spacing, buttons, and visual hierarchy all need to be on point and aligned to the guidelines. 3. Visuals (optional) Add high quality stock or AI-generated imagery/video to sections that currently have empty visual slots. You should have a strong eye for what works in a premium skincare context. STAGE TWO SCOPE - Home Page & Product Page. Both pages are already built and structured to the same level as the coming soon page. The scope mirrors stage one with the following additions: - Full branding pass across both pages consistent with the coming soon page - Product page requires knowledge of Shopify subscription apps - we have a specific subscription strategy that needs to be built and designed to match our requirements - A second version of the product page will need to be created for A/B testing with an alternate subscription layout - main different will be in the production information block (different subscription model to test) - Any missing sections built out and copy dropped in from provided brief - Visual placeholders replaced with final or high quality stock/AI imagery Stage two is not guaranteed. It is only offered if you perform in stage one. WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR - Shopify Liquid and theme customisation experience - Strong visual design sensibility - you can look at a page and instinctively know what's off - CRO awareness - you understand why elements are placed where they are and how to make them convert - Experience with Shopify subscription apps - we have not picked one yet. - Someone who can elevate an existing build without needing a brief for every decision - Clean communication and fast turnaround TO APPLY Please include: - 2-3 examples of Shopify stores or landing pages you've worked on — tell us exactly what you worked on with each example. We see a lot of people share websites with no explanation of what they actually did on them. - At least one example that shows your design sensibility, not just technical execution - When quoting, feel free to quote for stage one only or for both stages — stage two is not guaranteed and is only awarded based on performance in stage one

jobs.description: We're seeking a developer to build, in phases, an automated system covering inventory management → demand forecasting → factory purchase orders for our e-commerce business (Rakuten Ichiba + Shopify). Inventory sync is currently handled manually; automating this and building a data foundation is the starting point. The end goal is an AI system that forecasts demand from accumulated data and auto-generates purchase order drafts for our factories. ■ End Goal Accumulate inventory data in-house, forecast demand with AI, and auto-generate factory POs. Independence from third-party SaaS with data as our own asset is the core objective. ■ Development Phases Phase 1 has two scope options; please quote both. ----------------------------------------- PHASE 1-A — Lite: Ingestion + Master Inventory Only ----------------------------------------- Ingest orders from Rakuten and Shopify and decrement master inventory in our DB. No write-back to channels. Scope: - Rakuten order polling (scheduled) - Shopify order ingestion (webhook + polling redundancy, HMAC verified) - SKU-level master inventory decrement (event-sourced append-only log) - Admin UI: SKU mapping, inventory view, manual adjustment (3–4 screens) - Daily export to BigQuery Out of scope: inventory write-back to channels. Rakuten ↔ Shopify sync continues to be handled separately. ----------------------------------------- PHASE 1-B — Full: Two-way Sync with Write-back ----------------------------------------- Everything in 1-A, plus automated inventory push to both channels so they stay in sync. Additional scope: - Inventory push to Rakuten and Shopify (idempotent, retry, rate-limit handling) - Daily full reconciliation (re-push all SKUs) - Sync error queue UI + manual retry - Missing-mapping alerts - Slack notifications for critical errors - Admin UI expanded to 5–6 screens Goal: fully automated inventory sync + data foundation. This is the "CrossMall replacement" scope. ----------------------------------------- PHASE 2 — Visualization + Stockout Prediction Dashboards for SKU sales/inventory trends, low-stock alerts, stockout date prediction, lead-time-aware reorder alerts, stocktake and goods receipt, supplier master management. PHASE 3 — Recommended Orders + AI PO Draft Generation Demand forecasting, recommended order quantity (MOQ/lead-time/safety stock aware), per-factory PO draft auto-generation (PDF+CSV, EN/JA), forecast accuracy dashboard. Final approval and sending remain human-operated. ----------------------------------------- ■ When Applying, Please Include 1. Specific Rakuten RMS API + Shopify Admin API project examples 2. Effort + budget estimates for Phase 1-A, 1-B, 2, and 3 (Phase 3 requires ML experience; otherwise quote 1-2 only) 3. Earliest start date and weekly availability 4. Your experience with AI coding tools 5. Portfolio / GitHub Phase 1-only engagement is welcome. We may add Phase 2/3 based on results. ■ Expected Tech Stack Python 3.11+ / FastAPI, PostgreSQL (Cloud SQL), Cloud Run + Cloud Tasks + Secret Manager, BigQuery (already in use), Retool or FastAPI + Jinja2 + Tailwind for admin UI, Rakuten RMS API + Shopify Admin GraphQL API. Stack proposals welcome. ■ Required Phase 1-B: Hands-on Rakuten RMS API experience (order fetch + inventory update), Shopify Admin API experience (GraphQL preferred), PostgreSQL (transactions, UNIQUE constraints, row-level locking), solid understanding of idempotency/retry/consistency, GCP experience. Phase 1-A only: Python/FastAPI experience, either Shopify webhook or Rakuten order API experience, PostgreSQL basics, GCP experience. ■ AI Coding Tools Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot are welcome. However, inventory integrity logic must be human-reviewed, test coverage must be human-verified, and actual Rakuten/Shopify API behavior must be verified against real environments. ■ Working Setup Client: product decisions, API credentials, test data. Developer: design refinement, implementation, tests, documentation. Private GitHub repo. Hourly or monthly retainer preferred. Note: Rakuten RMS API documentation is Japanese-only with legacy SOAP/REST quirks. Please be upfront if you don't have direct Rakuten experience — it's essential for Phase 1-B. Questions-only inquiries welcome.
jobs.title: Rakuten × Shopify Inventory → Demand Forecasting → AI PO Generation — Phased Bid Request

jobs.description: About the role We're a direct-to-consumer health brand running high-volume paid traffic to conversion-focused content pages — advertorials, listicles, comparison pages, quiz funnels, and sales pages. Our growth depends on how fast and how cleanly we can ship new pages and iterate on live ones. We're looking for a developer who lives inside Funnelish, Replo, and Shopify and can turn briefs into shipped pages without hand-holding. What you'll actually do Build advertorial pages, listicles, and editorial-style landing pages (primarily Replo on Shopify, sometimes Funnelish) Ship new sales pages, PDPs, and quiz funnels from design briefs or rough references Iterate fast on live pages — section swaps, layout fixes, mobile polish, conversion tweaks Build reusable custom HTML/CSS/JS components that work cleanly inside Replo and Shopify (countdown timers, bundle selectors, trust bars, low-stock alerts, etc.) Small Shopify backend work when needed: theme edits, Flows, notification templates This is execution, not greenfield design. You're building against briefs and references — cleanly, quickly, and with minimal back-and-forth. Who we're looking for You have real, shipped work in Funnelish, Replo, and Shopify. Revenue pages, not demos. You can show us live URLs. You're genuinely fast. A section rewrite is hours, not days. A full advertorial build is days, not weeks. You solve your own problems. When something breaks in Replo or a Shopify theme throws a conflict, you don't escalate — you debug, fix, and ship. You communicate like an adult. Short, direct messages. When something's blocked, we hear about it the same day. You take pride in clean, mobile-first work. Pages don't break when a Liquid variable is missing. Layouts don't collapse below 400px. Non-negotiables on availability We operate in European hours (DACH market). We need someone who responds within a few hours during the working day and same-day always. If our baseline rhythm for messages is 24+ hours, this won't work. We're not asking for 24/7 — we're asking for reliability during normal working hours. What fast execution means here Standard advertorial page build: 2–4 days Section or component rewrite: same day or next day Urgent fixes on live pages: within hours A new sales page from brief to launch: under a week for most projects If those numbers feel aggressive to you, we're probably not a fit. What you get Steady recurring work — we're building and iterating constantly, not one-off gigs Briefs with clear direction (we know what we want and how to communicate it) Competitive rates for the right person, paid on time every time A long-term working relationship if it clicks Not a fit if You've dabbled in Replo or Funnelish but haven't shipped production work on them You need detailed spec documents for every task You routinely go quiet for 24+ hours You're managing five other clients and we'd be number six To apply Send us: 2–3 live page links you built on Replo, Funnelish, or Shopify (health/supplement/DTC a bonus) Your typical turnaround for a standard advertorial-style page Your hourly or project rate Your working hours and time zone

jobs.description: TITLE: Shopify Horizon theme setup — 5 digital PDFs via Digital Downloads app — 6hr turnaround — clinical fertility brand BUDGET: $100 fixed · 6-hour delivery Not a dropshipping store. No other pinks, no gradients, no lifestyle-pastel variants. The brand is clinical, not cute.Typography: Horizon defaults are fine (clean sans-serif). Do not install custom fonts unless specifically asked.Exact deliverables — 6-hour window: Install and activate Horizon theme Apply brand color palette above in Horizon theme editor (Theme settings → Colors) Connect custom domain (DNS managed via Cloudflare — domain details sent post-hire) Connect Stripe and PayPal as payment processors Install Digital Downloads app (free, native Shopify) Upload 5 digital PDF products (I will supply the PDFs, titles, descriptions, prices, cover images) Create one collection: "Fertility" — ladder order: RI Audit, Supplements, Nutrition, Uterine, Roadmap Configure checkout so buyers receive automated PDF delivery after purchase Test end-to-end: test purchase of free lead magnet, test purchase of paid product, verify download links work Basic homepage with hero section and featured collection (no custom design — Horizon defaults using my palette are fine) Mobile responsive check What I am NOT asking for: Custom theme development Custom CSS beyond Horizon's built-in color/style settings Logo or brand design (logo will be supplied) Copywriting Email marketing setup (already configured separately) Ongoing retainer Brand context (so you don't propose the wrong thing):The brand is fertility and reproductive health. Horizon's default layout is a good fit; use my palette, do not add decorative elements.To be considered, your proposal must include all three: Links to 2 Shopify stores you have built that sell digital products (not physical goods, not POD, not dropshipping). If you do not have 2 digital-product store examples, do not apply. Your specific Horizon theme experience — which Horizon features you have worked with, any known gotchas. Confirmation you can start within 1 hour of hire and deliver within 6 hours. Screening rules — please read before applying: Do not pitch SendOwl, Sky Pilot, FetchApp, or any paid digital delivery app. The spec is Digital Downloads (native, free). If you recommend an alternative, I will assume you did not read this post. Do not ask "is the budget flexible?" or offer a "priority turnaround upgrade." The budget is fixed at $100. The 6-hour turnaround is the spec, not an upsell. Do not pitch Shopify migrations, theme development, SEO services, CRO, or ongoing management. Those are not this job. Do not send generic templated proposals. I will read every proposal and generic ones get ignored. First line of your proposal should be the Shopify store URL of one digital-product store you have built. If your first line is "Hi, I hope this message finds you well" or any variation, I will not read the rest.Payment: $100 fixed-price milestone, released on confirmed working end-to-end test purchase. No hourly, no partial milestones, no scope expansion.SKILLS TAGS: Shopify · Shopify Horizon · Digital Downloads · Stripe · PayPal · Cloudflare · digital products

jobs.description: Hey, I’m building a new brand called NexClean — we’re launching eco-friendly detergent sheets designed for modern homes. This isn’t just another product; the goal is to build a clean, premium, global-feel brand inspired by Kind Laundry, with a strong focus on simplicity, quality, and user experience. I already have a clear design direction and a detailed mockup ready (will share), and now I’m looking for someone who can bring it to life properly on Shopify. I’m looking for someone who truly understands clean, minimal design (no clutter, no template feel), premium brand execution (spacing, typography, visual balance), and conversion-focused thinking (not just “good-looking” design). This is not a basic Shopify setup — attention to detail really matters here. The scope includes building a Shopify store using a theme like “Sense” (or similar), customizing it properly (not just drag-and-drop sections), and translating my mockup into a polished, responsive website. You’ll be working on the homepage (highest priority), product page (clean and conversion-focused), about page, and contact page. The homepage should feel simple, calm, and premium, with a strong product-first hero section, clear and focused messaging, thoughtful use of white space, and a smooth mobile experience. I’ll provide the logo, final color palette, product images, reference website and mockup, and clear structure so you won’t have to guess. From your side, I expect strong attention to detail (spacing, alignment, consistency), solid Shopify experience, the ability to think from a customer’s perspective, and clear, fast communication. The timeline is around 5–8 days, and the budget is open depending on experience — I’m not looking for the cheapest option, but the right fit. To apply, please share links to live Shopify stores you’ve built, examples of clean and premium work, and a short note on how you would approach this project. Bonus if you’ve worked with D2C or lifestyle brands. If this goes well, there’s potential for ongoing work like landing pages and updates. Please start your proposal with “NexClean” so I know you’ve read this. Looking forward to working with someone who genuinely cares about design quality 🙌
jobs.title: Shopify Designer Needed for Premium Eco Brand (Clean, Minimal, High-End)
